---
title: "Research Design: Quantitative and Qualitative"
---

## Learning goals
- Build credible research designs for antitrust questions using both quantitative and qualitative evidence.
- Select and combine diff-in-diff, event studies, IV, matching, RDD, and panel models with case facts.
- Design qualitative work (surveys, interviews, document analysis) that aligns with empirical tests.

## Workflow
- Scoping memo: question, theory of harm, data sources, timelines, key assumptions.
- Data pipeline: ingestion (`data/raw`), cleaning (`data/derived`), reproducible scripts (`scripts/`, `R/`).
- Pre-analysis plan for litigation: outcomes, treatment, controls, robustness, and disclosure of limitations.
- Citations: attach design choices to precedent (e.g., merger retrospectives [@ashenfelter_hosken_2010], class cert common impact [@dickey_rubinfeld_2014]) and note jurisdictional applicability.

::: {.callout-note title="Method box: Causal tools"}
- Diff-in-diff and event study diagnostics (parallel trends tests, dynamic leads/lags).
- Instrumental variables for supply/demand shocks; relevance and exclusion in antitrust contexts.
- Panel estimators (FE, two-way FE pitfalls, staggered adoption corrections).
- Synthetic control and matrix completion for single treated units.
- Clustering and randomization inference; wild bootstrap for few clusters.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Qualitative methods box"}
- Survey design for consumer substitution and diversion; sample frames and bias checks.
- Case studies: structured comparisons across firms/markets to triangulate conduct.
- Coding documentary evidence: templates for internal emails, strategy docs, board materials.
- Expert elicitation: when to use industry experts and how to document their priors.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Debate"}
- How much weight to give stated preference surveys vs. revealed behavior.
- Limits of parallel trends in markets with rapid innovation or multi-sided dynamics.
- When placebo/negative tests are persuasive in court.
:::

## Code box: diff-in-diff scaffold
```{r}
#| label: did-scaffold
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(fixest)
library(did)

# expected data columns: id, time, treat (0/1), outcome, controls...
# att_gt <- att_gt(yname = "outcome", tname = "time", idname = "id",
#                  gname = "treat_time", data = panel_data, panel = TRUE)
# summary(att_gt)

# Sun & Abraham style with fixest:
# feols(outcome ~ i(time, treat, ref = REF_PERIOD) + controls | id + time, data = panel_data, cluster = ~id)
```
